title: Federal FOIA Request — SSA, Massachusetts DDS Records (Sharpened)
description: Ready-to-file Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552) request to SSA, focused on the Massachusetts DDS records a state PRR cannot reach. Section 1 requests the SSA/ODD annual budget AUTHORIZATION/spending-authority issued to MA DDS (the per-state allocation not published in SSA's Congressional Justification) plus the SSA-4513 reimbursement actually paid, FY2013-2024. Section 2 requests State Agency Operations Report (SAOR) workload & staffing for all states FY2019-2024 (to benchmark MA). Section 3 requests the count of MA cases the SSA regional QA component returned for correction, by deficiency type. Section 4 requests SSA Boston Regional Office (Region I) performance-feedback letters and any performance-improvement-plan/corrective-action notices to MA DDS. Section 5 requests national-systems records (incoming-fax failure rate + NCPS template-edit process; DCPS rollout/downtime as it affected MA). Section 6 requests OIG audit 072309 and successors. Includes a §552(a)(4)(A)(iii) public-interest fee waiver and non-commercial requester category. Written in an individual capacity; never from an @ssa.gov address. Supersedes prr-templates/ssa-foia.
geographic_coverage: United States (all state DDS for SAOR benchmarking); Massachusetts DDS focus; SSA ODD + Boston Region I

source.publisher: U.S. Social Security Administration — Office of Privacy and Disclosure, FOIA (request target)
source.title: SSA FOIA — Freedom of Information Act (5 U.S.C. § 552)

request_status.tracking_note: Marked submitted per user instruction; confirmation/control number not recorded in repo.

update_cadence: As needed (revise FY ranges and audit numbers before each filing).
notes: Confidence tier = Authoritative because this file IS the canonical, repo-maintained FOIA request template/form. Use this rather than a MA state PRR for the cells docs/methodology.md flags 'structurally unavailable' — the SSA/ODD per-state DDS budget ALLOCATION is not published at state level and is unreachable via Chapter 66. Supersedes the older prr-templates/ssa-foia (which had a deliberately broken sentinel email address); this version points to the official SSA FOIA portal, FOIA liaison email, and Baltimore mailing address. Requester contact fields are filled for Wardis Vizcaino using a personal, non-@ssa.gov email address. References OIG audit 072309 (carded as ssa/oig-audit-staffing-productivity-072309).
